question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has been officially released by the BLS, showing CPI increased exactly 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The market appears to be pricing in outdated expectations or confusion about the actual data.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI rose 0.3%, well below the 0.9% threshold
Market closes April 10, 2026, the same day the data was released
No possibility of revision that would change the monthly increase from 0.3% to above 0.9%
Market pricing at 31% suggests significant mispricing given the definitive data
risks: Potential data revision, though monthly CPI revisions are typically minimal
Misinterpretation of the resolution criteria or data source
Market may not have fully processed the official release yet
